data.body: This “Trojan” moon, sharing an orbit with Dione, is a frozen time capsule. Helene features depressions and a smooth surface patterned with strange streaks and grooves, oriented down-gradient. Although this is still a matter of debate, they may result from landslides or the collapse of porous ice.

data.body: The Titanic had its own newspaper on board, The Atlantic Daily Bulletin. Printed daily, it included news, stock prices, horse-racing results, society gossip, and adverts—giving first-class passengers a taste of normal life even in the middle of the Atlantic Ocean.

I agree with almost all of what you've said. 

We do a baseline early doors in year 12 though, and I think it's a good idea.

The point isn't to punish, but to check the basics.
